steps.title: Pull out the key tension
steps.content: Researchers want to evaluate different conservation strategies, but they can't accurately count the langurs, so they can't tell whether the population is increasing, decreasing, or staying stable.

steps.title: Make the inference
steps.content:
If the team can't reliably measure population change, they can't reliably tell whether a strategy is working — so the study risks drawing inaccurate conclusions about the effectiveness of different strategies.

B and C add unsupported new ideas (other methodologies, other species). D suggests overlooking other approaches, which the passage doesn't imply.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
